context: Indonesian maritime logistics fleet serving inter-island energy and bulk cargo routes.

primaryChart.title: Fuel Consumption vs Weather by Vessel (Monthly)

secondaryChart.title: Engine Running Hours by Operational Mode

dashboardAnnotation: From 15 hours of manual reporting — to this.

chatScripts.userQuery: Why is Vessel 3's fuel consumption higher this month?
chatScripts.aiResponse: Vessel 3's fuel consumption is 18% above average this month. Analysis of the voyage logs shows three contributing factors: rough sea conditions on routes to Balikpapan (7 days), extended port waiting time at Tanjung Priok (average 14 hours vs fleet average 6 hours), and one documented engine maintenance delay.
followUps: What can we do about port delays?

chatScripts.userQuery: What can we do about port delays?
chatScripts.aiResponse: Port delays are concentrated at Tanjung Priok, where Vessel 3 averaged 14 waiting hours across 6 calls this month. Securing fixed berthing windows and pre-clearance can reduce waiting time to about 8 hours, cutting idle engine hours by 11% and lowering monthly fuel burn by roughly 9 MT.

The excess 36 MT fuel burn and 48 idle engine hours on Vessel 3 add about IDR 1.2B this month, equal to 6.5% of fleet operational cost. Estimated impact split: rough weather IDR 0.46B, port waiting IDR 0.52B, and maintenance delay IDR 0.22B.

recommendations.headline: Renegotiate port slot allocation at Tanjung Priok
recommendations.rationale: Based on: 28 days of port log data

recommendations.headline: Schedule Vessel 3 preventive maintenance before Q3
recommendations.rationale: Based on: engine hours and maintenance history

recommendations.headline: Adjust route planning during monsoon window
recommendations.rationale: Based on: weather correlation across 12 months
